laws.title: Public Law 32-232 | The Federal Pay Standardization Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 5 directly amends 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) with a new GS-1 through GS-15 pay table, while Sections 1-4 and 6-7 are uncodified ancillary provisions. No later-law repeal was identified in the materials reviewed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, definitions, and findings.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Federal Pay Standardization Act"; SEC 2 defines terms; SEC 3 states Congress finds that...

provisions.text_summary: Establishes an Office of Personnel Management framework and director structure.
provisions.evidence: There is established as an independent establishment in the executive branch an Office of Personnel Management.
provisions.notes: No determinable numbered Code host is identified in the source text.

provisions.text_summary: Replaces the 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) General Schedule table with a single-rate GS-1 through GS-15 table.
provisions.exact_change: Strike the existing General Schedule table in subsection (a) and insert the enacted grade/pay table for GS-1 through GS-15.
provisions.evidence: Section 5(a) says 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) "is amended by striking the General Schedule and inserting the following".

provisions.text_summary: Implementation and regulations for the new pay system.
provisions.evidence: The Director of the Office of Personnel Management shall take such actions as may be necessary to implement the amendments made by this Act.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective-date clause.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall become effective immediately following a majority approval by both the Senate and House of Representatives along with the signature of the President of the United States.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is anchored at Title 5 section 101 and includes a quoted-content dump, but the required textual rewrite is 5 U.S.C. 5332(a).

recommended_actions: Apply the 5 U.S.C. 5332(a) text replacement.
Keep the OPM framework and implementation language as concise statutory-note material if retained.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link from the note.

laws.title: Public Law 32-233 | Literally Introducing Taxes (LIT)
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 5 repeals 26 U.S.C. 1 and replaces it with a flat 20 percent tax on the taxable income of every individual. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be referred to by its short title, the "Literally Introducing Taxes" act or "LIT".

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 26 U.S.C. 1 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Mark section 1 for repeal and replacement.
provisions.evidence: SEC 5(1) says "26 U.S. Code § 1 shall be repealed in its entirety."

provisions.text_summary: Imposes a flat 20 percent tax on the taxable income of every individual.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the prior rate structure with the enacted flat 20 percent individual tax.
provisions.evidence: SEC 5(2) says, "There is hereby imposed on the taxable income of every individual a flat tax of 20%."

current_implementation.detail: The current XML carries a section-1 note and an amendment-history note, but it does not perform the required 26 U.S.C. 1 rewrite in positive text.

recommended_actions: Replace 26 U.S.C. 1 with the enacted flat-tax text.
Retain the short-title and effective-date language only as concise note material.
Trim the quoted-content dump and remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 32-234 | Intelligence Appointment Transparency and Oversight Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 adds new reporting and documentation requirements to 50 U.S.C. 3041, and Section 4 expands the list of positions requiring DNI concurrence under subsection (b)(2). No later-law repeal was identified in the materials reviewed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and definitions.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Intelligence Appointment Transparency and Oversight Act".

provisions.text_summary: Adds DNI nonconcurrence notice, consultation certification, and appointment documentation provisions to 50 U.S.C. 3041.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new reporting and documentation provisions into section 3041, including notice to the intelligence committees, consultation certifications, and appointment reporting.
provisions.evidence: Section 3 says the listed provisions are "added to §3041 of the current law".

provisions.text_summary: Expands the list of positions requiring DNI concurrence under 50 U.S.C. 3041(b)(2).
provisions.exact_change: Add the enumerated intelligence and military intelligence leadership positions to the concurrence list.
provisions.evidence: Section 4(a) states that section 3041(b)(2) "is amended to include the following additional positions".

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is anchored to Title 50 section 1, but the source requires textual amendments to 50 U.S.C. 3041 and a revised subsection (b)(2).

recommended_actions: Apply the 50 U.S.C. 3041 textual amendments.
Move or replace the note at the correct section 3041 anchor.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 32-235 | Command Responsibility And Noncompliance Enforcement (CRANE) Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. The act is a generally applicable military policy and enforcement enactment with no determinable positive-law Code host or express Code amendment. It remains operative as current statutory history.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be referred to as the "Command Responsibility and Noncompliance Enforcement Act" or "CRANE Act."

provisions.text_summary: Commanding-officer accountability and monthly reporting requirements.
provisions.evidence: Commanding Officers are responsible for everything that happens in their unit; every CO must submit a monthly report.

provisions.text_summary: Mandatory training and service-headquarters oversight/enforcement.
provisions.evidence: Every new Commanding Officer will go through mandatory training; Service Headquarters will review the actions of Commanding Officers regularly.

provisions.text_summary: Whistleblower protection, congressional reporting, and severability.
provisions.evidence: Any military member who reports misconduct in good faith will be protected from retaliation; SEC 10 is severability.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is on-topic for Title 10, but it is bloated with a full-text dump and Trello link instead of a concise historical note.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise military statutory note.
Do not fabricate a positive-law Code section.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 32-236 | Military Operational Authority Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 4 amends 10 U.S.C. 153 and Section 5 amends 10 U.S.C. 154; Section 3 merely restates existing 10 U.S.C. 152(c) language without changing it. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and definitions.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Military Operational Authority Reform Act".

provisions.text_summary: Restates the current 10 U.S.C. 152(c) Chairman rule without changing the section text.
provisions.evidence: Annex "C" within U.S.C. 152 defines the Chairman ... may not exercise military command over the Joint Chiefs of Staff or any of the armed forces.
provisions.notes: No textual amendment to 10 U.S.C. 152 is needed.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 10 U.S.C. 153 by adding Chairman operational-authority language.
provisions.exact_change: Add the enacted operational-authority language as a new subsection within section 153.
provisions.evidence: SEC 4 says U.S.C 153 "shall be added" with the Chairman's functions language.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 10 U.S.C. 154 and adds Vice Chairman functions language.
provisions.exact_change: Revise subsection (f) and add the Vice Chairman functions text as a new subsection.
provisions.evidence: SEC 5 says the Vice Chairman may exercise military command ... and creates an additional Annex E for Vice Chairman functions.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note sits at a title-10 history anchor, but the source requires actual textual amendments to sections 153 and 154; Section 3 should remain note-only.

recommended_actions: Apply the 10 U.S.C. 153 and 154 amendments.
Leave Section 3 as a concise historical note only.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 33-238 | Conference Oversight of Discipline and Ethics Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. The act establishes the ULPA, repeals prior public laws, and sets out a general legal-licensing framework. It remains operative in the supplied archive and is not shown as wholly repealed by later materials reviewed here.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Conference Oversight of Discipline and Ethics Act", or as the "CODE Act".

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 25-184, keeps PL 15-97 repealed, and voids bar-association replacement laws.
provisions.exact_change: Mark PL 25-184 as repealed in full and preserve PL 15-97 title I as already repealed.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 25-184 shall be repealed in its entirety.
provisions.notes: The bar-association nullification clause is uncodified history.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions, ULPA establishment, and licensure structure.
provisions.evidence: There shall be a Uniform Legal Practice Authority (ULPA) which shall be established as an independent agency within the Judicial branch.

provisions.text_summary: Ethical rules, education/accreditation, scope of practice, and offenses/enforcement.
provisions.evidence: The ULPA shall prescribe and publish a comprehensive Professional Code of Conduct; unauthorized practice is penalized.

provisions.text_summary: Office-abeyance, miscellaneous powers, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: The license ... shall be deemed to be held in abeyance ... This Act shall become effective upon enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is parked at a Title 50 anchor that does not match the subject matter, and it embeds a full-text dump instead of a concise history note.

recommended_actions: Keep the act as a statutory note only.
Relocate it away from the Title 50 anchor to a judicial/legal-practice anchor if one is used.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-239 | Judicial Recall Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. The act repeals Public Law 26-190 section 108(c) and replaces it with a new temporary-assignment framework for former judges and justices. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Judicial Recall Act".

provisions.text_summary: Repeals section 108(c) of Public Law 26-190.
provisions.exact_change: Strike section 108(c) of PL 26-190 in full.
provisions.evidence: Section 108, subsection C of Public Law 26-190 is repealed in its entirety.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the new temporary-assignment rule and removal terms for former judges and justices.
provisions.evidence: Following a majority concurrence ... the Conference may ... place such a person on temporary assignment to the United States District Courts.
provisions.notes: Source amends prior public-law text rather than a numbered U.S. Code section.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective-date clause.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall become effective upon enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is again parked at a Title 50 anchor and carries a full-text dump, but the substantive repeal-marking should be tied to PL 26-190 section 108(c).

recommended_actions: Record the PL 26-190 section 108(c) repeal at the source-law home.
Keep the temporary-assignment rule as concise history/note material only.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-240 | Literally Fixing Taxes
laws.status_basis: The source text is complete and reviewable, but the archive classifies this law as a repealed-recorded historical enactment. It is a later amendment to PL 32-233's tax language, and the current XML note treats it as retained solely for statutory history.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Literally Fixing Taxes" act or "LFT".

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 32-233 section 5(2) to raise the flat tax to 28 percent.
provisions.exact_change: Change the flat tax rate from 20% to 28%.
provisions.evidence: Section 5(2) of H.R. 9 is hereby amended to read: "There is hereby imposed ... a flat tax of 28%."
provisions.notes: Treat as historical because the archive records the law as superseded.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals conflicting tax-rate language to the extent of the inconsistency.
provisions.evidence: All language in H.R. 9 or any other Act establishing a tax rate inconsistent with this amendment is hereby repealed to the extent of the inconsistency.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML treats this law as statutory history rather than live positive text, which matches the archive classification; the remaining cleanup is the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Keep this enactment as a historical note only.
Do not revive it as live positive-law text unless a later source requires it.
Trim the quoted-content dump and remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-241 | Protecting Working Americans
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. Section 3 directly revises the pay scale established in the prior pay-standardization law and should be applied as a textual amendment to the existing pay section. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and severability.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Protecting Working Americans Act".

provisions.text_summary: Revises the GS pay scale in the prior pay-standardization act.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the prior pay table with the new GS-15 through GS-1 pay amounts listed in the source.
provisions.evidence: Section 5(a) of the Federal Executive & Senior Leadership Pay Standardization Act of 2025 "shall be amended to read" the new schedule.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective-date clause.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall take effect immediately upon its passage ... and signature by the President of the United States.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note sits at a Title 46 anchor, but the source requires a textual rewrite of the pay table in the earlier pay-standardization section.

recommended_actions: Apply the revised pay table as a textual amendment to the underlying pay section.
Do not leave the change as note-only history.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 34-242 | Federal Courthouse Security & Jurisdiction Act
laws.status_basis: Source text is complete and reviewable. The act is a local/facilities-specific jurisdiction and security measure with no express positive-law Code amendment, so it is best preserved as statutory-note material rather than permanent section text. No later-law repeal was identified in the reviewed materials.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the "Federal Courthouse Security and Jurisdiction Act" or "FCSJ".

provisions.text_summary: Defines DOJ headquarters and D.C. federal courthouse property lines.
provisions.evidence: The property lines of the United States Department of Justice headquarters ... shall be understood to include...

provisions.text_summary: Grants U.S. Marshal Service jurisdiction, security authority, road-closure power, and notice requirements.
provisions.evidence: The United States Marshal Service shall have full authority and jurisdiction over the premises defined in this Act.

current_implementation.detail: The current anchor is plausibly related to Title 40, but the note still contains a quoted-text dump and should be reduced to a concise facilities/jurisdiction history note.

recommended_actions: Keep the enactment as a concise statutory note.
Do not create a fabricated positive-law section amendment.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
